reviews.review_msg: The class is very confusing, unorganized and prepare to teach yourself everything!

He makes this SCM class quite tough for no reason. He's a great guy, but not a very good professor; disorganized, often late, no feedback on projects, etc. Also, he presents only qualitative content while the test is heavily quantitative. Not terribly difficult, but no one knew how to calculate anything so the average was quite low.

Contents wise, it's straight forward. His quizzes and exams are not considered extremely hard; but going into final exam unprepared is difficult. Had we been given a few more practice scenarios, results could be significantly higher!

As an upcoming BBA graduate in Operations Management, I was really excited to steep myself into Supply Chain Management. Unfortunately, the instructor was not very engaging in class, content was purely theorical with very few calculation-based exercises from which the exam was 40% of final grade. I would avoid taking him in any class.

He failed to mention until a few days before our final (worth 40% of grade) that it would be made up of mainly calculation/long answer questions. He had never walked us through a quantitative problems, did not give us opportunities to practice calculations and all previous quizzes only tested on theory. Never replies to emails either

Prof teaches at UBC Sauder, not even kidding, the slides have boxes that cover up important text and other material. There is literally 0 consideration for the students when the materials are so disorganized. Not to mention that simple email replies take many days to receive, seriously? I don't know how nobody else has complained about this! Wow

Prof started teaching at UBC Sauder - Class is stale and difficult to follow along with. Quizzes are tough and extremely nuanced; lectures do not prepare you to do well and you are required to self-study the textbook. Class discussions dive deep into nuances not relevant for later examination. I would not recommend this course.

I'm sharing this feedback constructively. Prof. began teaching at UBC Sauder, but the course experience does not yet meet standards. A static lecture style makes it hard to stay engaged, group details are collected by email rather than Canvas, and a 45-minute presentation was assigned with one week's notice without sufficient class foundation.
The course was practical, and I enjoyed attending his lectures! The assessment and overall mark were satisfactory, although the exams were challenging. He is lacking in responsiveness to emails!

He 100% writes his own reviews. All the bad reviews are completely accurate. He doesn't teach anything. The averages are around 40%. He blames the class for the low averages. This class felt satire. Just don't do it!!!
Knowledgeable and clear in the subjects. Heavy emphasis on both final and midterm exams. MC questions are challenging!
He makes the topics easy to understand, I really enjoyed taking this class with him.
Very nice guy.

Overall, a somewhat nice and pretty funny guy. Midterm was bad, but final was a lot easier, mainly because out of 5, the first 3 written questions were copied word for word from the midterm, and the fourth question was word for word number for number copied from the last assignment. He should provide more practice problems to help students learn.

yall im being so deadass when i say avoid unless your amazing at teaching urself. everything we learn in class is using excel&tableau software but the exams are only handwritten and he doesn't teach u how to do anything by hand u learn on ur own. also doesn't provide any sample questions that reflect the only handwritten method all of them use exel

lectures are long and unhelpful. he leaves stuff out of the posted slides, so you should definitely attend class. the practice exam sample questions are only available in class, and your cheat sheet can't have graphs or notes. he didn't post an assignment/quiz so he made them optional. not the worst but disappointing considering he's a recent grad.

He does not know how to teach. Gives no problems or homework to practice for midterms and exams- was so lazy he gave everyone automatically 10% for an assignment he didn't post. You will have to teach yourself everything. All work we did was through Excel and on midterm he expected us to calculate by hand, never teaching us how to.
does not know how to use a computer.

Honestly, the content for this course is not hard, he just teaches it awfully. The midterm average was 41%, with the best mark around 75% in a class of 200 ppl. No practice questions, he pushes back everything, doesn't answer questions properly and teaches terribly. (I did good on the exam so I am not writing this out of spite).
Mehdi please do not pretend to be a student and rate yourself 5 here, you know you don't deserve it

I'm kinda blown away by some unfavorable comments, all because the midterm was a letdown! although some of the questions were basically copy-pasted from the assignments. Accustomed to detailed exam instructions, challenging questions lead us to blame. But honestly, Mehdi's a cool, smart teacher, totally the right person for this course.

Avoid this man at all costs. He takes a second to look at his slides, then proceeds to "explain" them to the class by reading word for word off of the slides. It's almost as if he's learning the concepts along with the class:) He also doesn't know how to project his voice and gets too lazy to hold the mic up so no one can hear what he's saying.

Dear Mehdi,

I hope this comment finds you bad. You are the worst.

Best,

Your 336 Student

This guy has many jobs at the same time and professor is just one of his job. He has a HUGE PROBLEM with his TIME MANAGEMENT. 
Mehdi, I don't know if you see the comment here. But please! If you cannot manage this, just do not be a professor! Do not waste our tuition! Do not destroy our lives! You have a serious problem with teaching!

Never in my 4 years of uni have I witnessed a 40% EXAM AVG, especially in a class of 200+. Exam was pushed back a week already, 1-page formula sheet was allowed, and an extra 20 mins was given halfway through the exam. 

dawg this should be the moment for serious reflection as a professor lol

sweaty ah classroom, could smell everyone's pit sweat.
A good instructor for this course offers very standard lectures, exams, assignments, and quizzes.

Knowledgeable, hilarious, easygoing, and punctual professor. The midterm included a few questions identical to the assignments, yet we did not expect coding in the exam. The course focuses on applied analytics using Tableau/Excel, yet the exam needs to be on paper, making it challenging to practice for. Overall Mehdi is nice professor.

Midterm was way too easy, I almost passed (average was 40%). 

If it wasn't for the 30 degree room making me delusional mixed with terrible teaching I may have actually learned something. He also doesn't understand his own slides and acts as if he's seeing them for the first time. Overall great prof! (Avoid at all costs)

Do not take this class. The professor is always late to class and told us that the textbook was examinable and most of the questions were from course lectures. He literally had us handwrite tableau code for the exam. Not enough coursework to learn the materials properly. Midterm average was 40% and he ignored our feedback.
Good teacher, engaging, and you actually learn real-world skills. The multiple-choice questions on the exams aren't as straightforward!

He makes ZERO announcements about anything, ever. He posts quiz and assignment due dates for the morning of the lecture day, then without warning the next assignment and quiz are suddenly due a day earlier, then again the assignment and quiz due dates are due another day earlier. He makes zero effort to let us know any changes in the schedule

He arrives late almost every day, and is often late to release assignments, giving us less time to do them. It's like he's seeing his powerpoints or videos for the first time and he has to verbally decipher them (which takes a while) during the class. Doesn't allow students to email him. Speaks very quietly and is hard to understand.

Nice enough guy and seems smart however is a terrible teacher. If you want to save yourself some time go to the first 2 lectures just so you can see for yourself how pointless it is. His slides and "examples" he uses in class are plagiarised (practically word for word). There's only a 350 character limit but to sum it up Chat GPT is a better teache

Hard to understand, makes content harder than needed. Very monotone and has been repeatedly told to raise voice in class which he blames on technical difficulties. He does try tho which I give him credit for.
Mehdi is a knowledgeable, organized, and easygoing professor.
Avoid.

Amazing teacher. He records his lectures and is really good at explaining the material. There was no final, just 2 midterms and they were not cumulative. Midterm's are pretty hard but he gives you a breakdown of what it includes so you know what to expect. Assignments helped a lot in boosting your grade & in preparing for midterms.

The course content was well-structured and presented using Arena. The hands-on approach to learning was effective. 2 group assignments and a group project, and the final exam was on modeling using Arena. Mehdi knows the content and he is very friendly with students.
Liked his course. MC questions in both midterm and final exams are challenging.

Mehdi is very good in teaching DE Simulation. He is a very nice and approachable professor. He encouraged us to think critically and helped us develop a deeper understanding of simulation modeling and its limitations. This is an applied course, and the final exam is to develop simulation models using Arena. I enjoyed it very much.

He used clear examples to illustrate the concepts we were learning. was very patient and approachable for questions. Exams are very similar to the quizzes and assignments, and usually 1 or 2 questions in the exams are identical to the ones that the professor works through during the lectures. He is a nice knowledgeable prof.

He explains everything in detail and makes it easier for me to learn the contents. He is always on-time, supportive, and very knowledgeable. He is a good teacher to take 232 with. He sometime is late responding emails.

Lectures are not mandatory, yet I found it helpful to attend. Assignments and quizzes were very similar to the midterm exam questions. Clear grading criteria. Standard slides with many examples from last years exams. A supportive and knowlagable teacher, yet TAs not always helpful.

Avoid this person at all costs, he simply does not want to work. I do not understand why he puts so little effort into making this course convenient for everyone. He would always come to class late, his slides were disorganized and he would finish them early, probably because he was tired. Although, I'm sure he's knowledgable, he cant teach

Accessible prof not by email but by his phone number. He was late to class by 10min once and was pretty apologetic about it. He cares enough about his class that he would consider our opinions about certain questions and adjusted the final questions on the exam to better align with what we learned in class.

Attendance isn't mandatory, and I would go because I wanted to do well in the course, but I decided to stop and teach myself becuas of his slides stright out of the texbook, and he has slow, monotone 4 hour lectures. Don't take a classs with him, I taught myself the whole class and did better without attending. He also has a no email policy
He is very kind teacher!! He is good!!
He is very good. I like him

Mehdi is a great choice for this class and explains concepts thoroughly and well. Very helpful assignments that prepare you for exams. If you understand how to do the questions proposed in assignments, you can guarantee an above average grade in this course. Overall, very fair exams and interesting lectures.
Mehdi always explains things clearly, very patient and a very nice person!
Standard PPT, and clear lectures. Mehdi knows the content pretty well and he is good in explaining them. Participation is not mandatory yet I recommend it.

He puts little to no effort into this class. He uses the slides that the textbook provides and refuses to post lecture recordings. He was 15-20 minutes late for every lecture.There was one question on the midterm that was not covered in the class and the highest mark for that question was 3/12. He refused to drop the question.
Mehdi is great in teaching Simulation. So knowledgeable in this field and I learned a lot in his class.
He is a great teacher. Knows the contents well and he makes sure you get them! I have learned so much in his class.

Mehdi is a caring lecturer and he is passionate for teaching stats. He is always prepared for the material and how to communicate it. Lectures are not mandatory, yet he provides more examples during the lectures which you may not find them in the slides posted on canvas. 4 assignments + 5 Quizzes count for almost 50% of the total grade.
worst lecturer in which only a quarter of the class actually attended.
Mehdi reviews many examples during the lectures which help to better digest the content. You do not need to memorize the formulas as a cheatsheet is allowed in the exams.

Mehdi is a very kind and intelligent man. He is open to questions and good at clearly explaining concepts. I really appreciate the time he took to teach us Tableau as it is an incredibly useful tool. Mehdi have been a wonderful professor and I hope that many others follow suit in going to extra mile to teach us relevant information.

Mehdi is a very understanding, genuine, and helpful professor who wants to see all of his students succeed, although making 250+ students happy with the course is impossible. Mehdi clearly explains each concept in a straightforward manner. Participation is not mandatory, so you can skip the lectures if you find the slides/text self-explanatory.

Very very poor explanation skills, I feel like I understand the concepts better before he actually starts explaining them. Way too much weight on online quizzes that are rigged anyways because everything's just on chegg.

He refuses to record lectures during a pandemic and you basically need to attend lectures or you will be lost. So, basically if you get covid you will miss a class or two of content. Also he doesn't respond to emails, not because he's bad at it. He has a policy that he does not respond to emails. His voice in monotone and he just lectures the text.

I'm sure Medhi is a nice guy in general, but he lacks many traits that make a good professor. One, he isn't accessible. It's always difficult to get a reply. Two, he mostly reads off the slides. He barely gives examples so get ready to read the book to answer the assignment questions. And three, he gives out information a few hours before the exam.

2 days before the final exam changed it from take home to in person. changed the schedule and duratin of exam about 5 times. really boring and dry lectures. says lectures are optional but theyre clearly mandatory and refuses to post recordings. really messed us up when he changed the final exam format 2 day before the final. avoid at all cost.

Decided 3 days before the exam that it would be a take-home that was outside the scheduled exam time, then changed it back to in-person the day before the exam. Incredibly unorganized, was behind the syllabus the entire semester so students never knew when things were due. Was late to every lecture and gave very unclear criteria.
One of the best professors at SFU. Go through his lecture notes, and you'll do well in this course. All the best.

Nice guy wants class to do well. Lectures are pointless since he just rambles on during the slides; you can get by just reading the textbook which I found more helpful. He goes over how to use excel and some question examples in lecture which is the thing to pay attention to. He NEVER replies to emails. NOT accessible outside of class.

Workload is a lot which is normal for 4units. Mehdi is a nice guy but a little out of touch with reality. Monotonous lecturing coupled with mandatory (synched with quiz, technically it is) attendance is a pain. Need lots of practice. Hi-stakes assignments with 1 attempt so you cant learn from your mistakes w/c may just be decimals but who knows.

This class is online and asynchronous for a reason, but he made attending lectures mandatory in order to do the quiz, which btw was 12 minutes for 15 questions. Changed the grading scheme midway through the semester and made assignment questions worth a ridiculous number of marks if you got the wrong answer. There are better profs.

Mehdi is a nice guy with a kind heart, but not the best prof. lectures consist of him reading off slides, but its the workload that makes it a hell mouth. weekly assignments worth 7% each will take you HOURS ON HOURS, and quizzes are 12 mins for 15 questions which will leave you sweating like a dog. only take this class with 2 other ones.

Mehdi is a good professor, however, his assignments were extremely long compared to the other 232 professors. His exams were quite fair and the average was higher than what I usually heard for 232. He went back on his grading system by changing the online quizzes to be done viz zoom proctoring and made them worth 15% less (added to assignments).

Assignments are way too long and too hard and he does not provide enough time to complete them. Only take this course with him if you have low course load. Does not provide enough time on quizzes and exams. Lectures are just him reading off the slide. Be prepared to self-learn everything.
Does not give enough time to complete quizzes, assignments, and homework. He is inaccessible to reach and in lecture all he does is read over slides.

He seems nice and knows the content well. What is bad is his due dates are way too early. He acts like this is the only course we are taking. I recommend not taking him unless you have a medium level course load (2-3 other classes in which are easy)

Not a good lecturer, mostly repeats whats on the slides and does Excel examples, and talks very quietly and holds the mic at weird spots so its hard to hear him most of the time. Spends a lot of the class teaching tableau, being the only prof for this class that does. Doesnt provide practice questions even though its so excel-heavy.

Medhi did a great job at teaching for the first time. Although it was a regurgitation of 336, he was good at explaining the concepts and demonstrating tableau. I hope for future students he introduces programs such as R. He's a good man and very helpful.
I love this guy. Really really nice prof, and even though it's hard to express appreciation to him during online lectures, I hope he sees this and knows he's doing a great job.
